mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS reduces discount rates and eases financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when the policy rate is falling from an already restrictive level.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than unconditional returns after FEDFUNDS declines over any 3-month window, or if the sign is negative in more than 60% of easing observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals labor-market deterioration and future earnings downside, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 20 trading days after the unemployment rate breaks materially higher.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 40% of cases, or if the median forward return is not below zero after the threshold is crossed.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth in RSAFS indicates resilient household demand and near-term revenue support, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month when sales are accelerating rather than decelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS uptrend months are not followed by higher 20-day SPX returns than downtrend months, or if the effect disappears in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in INDPRO signals stronger real activity and improving corporate output, which should support earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum does not coincide with above-median 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips negative in more than half of expansion episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST and GDPC1 both weaken, the economy is losing residential-led demand and broad activity, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next quarter because earnings revisions widen to the downside.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint deterioration in HOUST and GDPC1 fails to predict below-zero 60-day SPX returns, or if either series alone fully explains the effect without interaction terms.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS regime combined with elevated CPIAUCSL indicates restrictive real policy and inflation pressure, which compresses valuations and should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered high-rate/high-inflation periods do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the joint condition adds no predictive power beyond FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS trend with rising UNRATE signals broad labor-market slack and weaker future consumption, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 5 trading days when payroll growth turns decisively negative.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change < 0 and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if the combined payroll-loss and unemployment-rise signal does not lead to negative 5-day SPX returns in a majority of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-soft-landing rebound, rising GDPC1 and stable or lower UNRATE indicate broadening demand without labor stress, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if expanding GDP with stable unemployment does not correspond to above-zero 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect weakens to zero in recovery periods across rolling 3-year windows.
